heroHeadline: SAF Drone Kills RSF Politician in Nyala; UN: El Obeid Aid Suspended, Adré Crossing Extended to June
heroSubtitle: SAF conducted a targeted drone strike in Nyala on April 1, killing RSF-aligned Ta'asees founder Osama Hassan and narrowly missing RSF 'Prime Minister' Al Taishy; the UN extended the critical Adré border crossing through June 30, though Sudan's 2026 humanitarian plan remains only 16% funded ($2.9B needed); UN warned El Obeid medical services have been suspended ~1 month and all roads to Dilling/Kadugli are too dangerous for aid; 4,000+ more civilians fled Blue Nile State to Ethiopia in March as RSF-SPLM-N pressure continues near Geissan and Ed Damazin
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM
